channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: How Black leaders in New York are grappling with Eric Adams and representation
item.description: The indictment of New York City mayor Eric Adams on federal bribery charges has sparked debates among Black leaders and community advocates about whether to stand behind a figure who, for many, represents a milestone of Black representation in government.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Morrison And Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The flood of ghost guns is slowing after regulation. It's also being challenged in the Supreme Court
item.description: The Supreme Court is hearing arguments in a case over a federal regulation aimed at reducing weapons often referred to as ghost guns.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Prosecutors lay out new evidence in Trump election case, accuse him of having 'resorted to crimes'
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump laid the groundwork to try to overturn the 2020 election even before he lost, knowingly pushed false claims of voter fraud and “resorted to crimes” in his failed bid to cling to power, according to a court filing unsealed Wednesday that offers new evidence from the lan
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump calls new January 6th accusations 'pure election interference'
item.description: The former president spoke exclusively to NewsNation about the unsealing of documents related to his election interference case.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump on Springfield Haitian migrants: 'They have to be removed'
item.description: The former president spoke to NewsNation's Ali Bradley about his plans regarding the Haitian migrants living in Springfield, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Kaine and Cao face off in only debate of campaign for US Senate seat from Virginia
item.description: U.S. Sen. Tim Kaine of Virginia and Republican challenger Hung Cao have gone toe to toe in their only debate of the campaign over issues from illegal immigration to tariffs on foreign goods.
item.dc:creator: Ben Finley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Are Nevada voters swayed by promises of 'no tax on tips'?
item.description: Reporter John Langeler from KLAS in Las Vegas joins "The Hill" to discuss whether Nevada voters are swayed by proposals to eliminate federal taxes on tips.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Watch: FOX59/CBS4 Indiana Governor Debate
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS -- For the first time this election year, the two major party candidates are having the opportunity to participate in a televised debate surrounding issues that are important for Indiana voters. U.S. Senator Mike Braun, R-Ind.
item.dc:creator: David Gay, NewsNation

item.title: NJ lawmakers explore why summer energy bills were so high
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) - If your energy bills were sky-high this summer, you are not alone. It's why New Jersey lawmakers came together for a hearing to learn why and to discuss how to solve it.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Minneapolis grants consent for Blue Line Extension through city’s north side
item.description: The Minneapolis City Council signed off Wednesday on the proposed route of a light rail line that will run through a section of the city’s north side. However, some council members said they could only get to a yes vote after some of their concerns with the route were addressed.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump ups his ante with September fundraising haul
item.description: Former President Trump rakes in over $160 million in fundraising in September, up from his August haul, as he tries to catch up to Vice President Kamala Harris in the 2024 election cash dash.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans lead September voter registration charge in Nevada
item.description: Nonpartisan voters were just ahead of Republicans registering to vote in Nevada during September. Democrats lagged in third.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump rejects calls for another debate with Harris after VP showdown
item.description: Former President Donald Trump again rejected the prospect of another debate with Vice President Kamala Harris late Tuesday following a showdown between the two vice presidential nominees, saying he was "not looking to do it again.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says Walz 'embarrassed himself' compared to Vance's 'brilliance' at VP debate
item.description: Former President Trump applauded his running mate, Ohio Sen. JD Vance, for a “brilliant performance" during Tuesday night’s vice presidential debate against Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz in an exclusive interview with Fox News Digital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden, Harris inspect damage in hurricane-ravaged Southeast in wake of Trump visit
item.description: President Biden and Vice President Harris each travel to the storm-ravaged Southeast on Wednesday, two days after Donald Trump visited – as the death toll and devastation from Hurricane Helene soars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pennsylvania town grapples with Trump assassination attempt ahead of his return
item.description: BUTLER, Pa. (AP) — Donald Trump is going back to Butler, where the world last saw him pump his fist and beseech followers to “fight,” even as blood streaked his face from a would-be assassin's bullet.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Jersey Dem House candidate says she is 'not super worried' about biological men in women's locker rooms
item.description: A New Jersey Democrat and former women's basketball player running for Congress said she is "not super worried" about biological men being in women's locker rooms
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top 5 clashes between Vance and Walz during debate showdown: 'Your mics are cut'
item.description: Fox News Digital compiled the top five clashes between Ohio Sen. JD Vance and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz during their first and only vice presidential debate this cycle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Walz and Vance go in depth on policy while attacking each other’s running mates in VP debate
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In a debate that evoked a calmer era in American politics, Tim Walz and JD Vance on Tuesday went after each other's running mates and sought to shore up their campaigns' vulnerabilities at a time of renewed fears of a regional war in the Middle East and sadness over devastation from
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, CHRIS MEGERIAN, WILL WEISSSERT and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know on claims made about Minnesota, Walz in VP debate against Vance
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz’s home state of Minnesota came up early in his debate against fellow vice presidential candidate Ohio Sen. JD Vance on Tuesday evening. Here are the facts.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: NY race for US Senate: Gillibrand talks Adams indictment, term limits, economy
item.description: Come Nov. 5, the 15-year Democratic senator will defend her seat against Republican challenger Mike Sapraicone.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Democrat calls for Congress to return to pass disaster aid after Hurricane Helene
item.description: Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) on Tuesday called for the Senate to return from its lengthy preelection recess to pass a disaster aid package following the damage left in multiple states by Hurricane Helene. “I think we should pass it yesterday.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance, Tim Walz face off in 2024 VP debate: Key takeaways
item.description: Republican J.D. Vance and Democrat Tim Walz met Tuesday in the lone vice presidential debate of the 2024 election, bringing together undercards who have spent two months going after each other and the opposing nominees who top the major-party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Eric Adams appoints new chief counsel amid string of resignations
item.description: Prosecutors unsealed an indictment last week alleging Adams took bribes and illegal campaign contributions.
item.dc:creator: Armando Cataldi, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans eager to keep House control are open to Trump’s SALT reversal
item.description: Republicans are open to raising the state and local tax (SALT) deduction cap that the party championed and put in place under former President Trump seven years ago as they aim to win total control of government in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Photos: As Jimmy Carter celebrates his 100th birthday, a look through the decades
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter is turning 100. Here are images from notable events or parts of his life, both during his presidency and after.
item.dc:creator: James Doubek, MPR News

item.title: Walz, Vance split on abortion, border, economic policies in issues-driven debate
item.description: Questions around climate change, border concerns, abortion and the economy drove Tuesday night’s vice presidential faceoff. Vance and Walz remained largely cordial in the debate and steered away from personal attacks.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: JD Vance’s time on the trail has often been overshadowed by self-made controversy
item.description: Republican Ohio Sen. JD Vance is prolific on the campaign trail with speeches and talking to reporters, but his messaging has at times been overshadowed by controversy often of his own making.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: A century of Jimmy Carter: Longest-living former president turns 100
item.description: In 1998, at the ripe age of 74, former President Carter put some of his thoughts about getting older to paper. "The virtues of aging," Carter wrote in a book that year, "include both the blessings that come to us as we grow older and what we have to offer that might be beneficial to others.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Trump suspect pleads not guilty in attempted assassination
item.description: The man accused of attempting to assassinate former President Trump at one of his Florida golf courses earlier this month has pleaded not guilty to five federal charges, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Vance and Walz debate in New York City on Tuesday. Here’s what you need to know
item.description: The CBS News vice presidential debate will be the only time Ohio Sen. JD Vance and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz get to face off ahead of the 2024 election. Here's what to expect.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Lawmakers fear 'chaos' if Trump-Harris race goes to the wire
item.description: Lawmakers in both parties are bracing themselves for a messy aftermath to Election Day as polls show the race between former President Trump and Vice President Harris is so close in several battleground states that it could take days to determine the winner.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Kamala Harris presents case for rebuilding the middle class in Las Vegas campaign stop
item.description: Kamala Harris wrapped up her second visit to Las Vegas since she became the Democratic presidential candidate, focusing on multiple policy issues.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here are the rules for the Vance-Walz debate
item.description: The debate, which will kick off at 9 p.m. EDT on Tuesday from the network’s New York office, will be moderated by CBS anchor Norah O’Donnell and "Face the Nation" host Margaret Brennan.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: PIX Panel discusses historic indictment against Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – History was made in New York City this week when Eric Adams became the city’s first sitting mayor to be indicted on federal charges. A federal indictment charged Adams with bribery, campaign finance and conspiracy offenses.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Oregon senator introduces bill to reform Supreme Court, add 6 more seats
item.description: Oregon Sen. Ron Wyden introduced bill on Thursday that would bring fundamental changes to the nation's high court.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, Fox 5
